_sources.discrepancies: Utah is largely a vote-by-mail state under HB37 (2012) and subsequent legislation — most counties conduct elections entirely by mail. NCSL classifies Utah as non-photo ID requested (utility bill, paycheck, bank statement, or other documentation with name and address). The Utah primary is typically the last Tuesday in June; estimated 2026-06-30. 2026 registration deadlines estimated from Utah statute (30 days before for online/mail; 7 days before for late in-person via DMV-linked process). TODO verify exact 2026 cycle dates.

earlyVoting.notes: Most Utah counties are vote-by-mail — ballots mailed to all active registered voters. Some counties may have in-person voting locations. TODO verify exact 2026 dates and county-by-county details.

Most Utah counties conduct elections entirely by mail — ballots sent to all active registered voters approximately 21 days before Election Day. Vote centers may be available in some counties for in-person voting.

Most Utah counties are vote-by-mail — all active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot. Some counties may have vote centers for in-person voting. No traditional polling places in most counties. Non-photo ID is requested but signature and address matching are primary verification methods.

acceptedIds: Utah driver's license or state ID
Current utility bill with name and address
Bank statement with name and address
Government check or document with name and address
Any government-issued document with name and address
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Utah is predominantly a vote-by-mail state. For in-person voting at vote centers, phones are generally permitted for reference purposes. Photography of marked ballots may be restricted. Check with your county clerk for specific rules.
additionalRules: Most Utah counties conduct elections entirely by mail — active registered voters automatically receive a mail ballot.
Utah has a closed primary — only voters registered with a party may vote in that party's primary.
Non-photo ID is requested when voting in person, but identity can be established with various documents showing name and address.
Automatic voter registration is available via Utah DMV and online state services.

eligibility: All registered voters automatically receive a ballot by mail in counties that conduct vote-by-mail elections (most Utah counties)
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: No application required in vote-by-mail counties — ballots are automatically mailed to active registered voters
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by 8:00 p.m. on Election Day, or postmarked by Election Day and received within specified window
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Return by 8 p.m. on Election Day at a drop box or county office, or mail postmarked by Election Day.

primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEn: Utah uses a hybrid primary system (Utah Code Ann. §20A-9-408, SB54 2014). Candidates may qualify via caucus-convention or signature gathering. Primary elections are generally open to registered voters of the qualifying party and unaffiliated voters.
primaryParticipation.ruleExplanationEs: Utah utiliza un sistema primario híbrido (Utah Code Ann. §20A-9-408, SB54 2014). Los candidatos pueden calificar mediante el sistema de caucus-convención o mediante la recolección de firmas. Las elecciones primarias generalmente están abiertas a los votantes registrados del partido calificado y a los votantes sin afiliación.
